skill.name: wordpress-woocommerce-development
skill.description: WooCommerce store development workflow covering store setup, payment integration, shipping configuration, and customization.
skill.summary: Complete WooCommerce workflow for building online stores with payment gateways, shipping, and customization.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 38 patterns (35 external_commands, 3 weak crypto) but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The SKILL.md file is documentation-only markdown containing skill references and workflow instructions. Backticks are markdown code formatting for skill names like @app-builder, not shell execution. No cryptographic code exists - 'weak crypto' matched text in descriptions. No executable code or security risks present.

content.user_title: Build WooCommerce stores with complete setup and customization
content.value_statement: Creating WooCommerce stores requires managing multiple setup phases and integrations. This workflow guides you through store setup, payment integration, shipping configuration, and optimization systematically.
actual_capabilities: Guide through complete WooCommerce store setup and configuration
Integrate payment gateways including Stripe and PayPal
Configure shipping zones, methods, and carrier integration
Customize product pages, cart, and checkout templates
Install and configure WooCommerce extensions like subscriptions and bookings
Optimize store performance with caching, CDN, and database optimization
limitations: Requires WordPress installation with WooCommerce plugin access
Payment gateway configuration requires merchant account credentials
Cannot directly execute WordPress admin actions or install plugins
Advanced customization may require PHP development skills

use_cases.title: New online store launch
use_cases.description: Set up a complete WooCommerce store from scratch including products, payments, and shipping configuration for a small business launching their first ecommerce site.

use_cases.title: Payment gateway integration
use_cases.description: Add or switch payment processors like Stripe or PayPal to an existing WooCommerce store with proper testing and configuration.

use_cases.title: Store optimization and scaling
use_cases.description: Improve performance of an existing WooCommerce store through database optimization, caching setup, CDN configuration, and checkout flow testing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic store setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up a new WooCommerce store. I need to install WooCommerce, configure basic settings for my product type, set up tax rules for my region, and configure the store currency.

prompt_templates.title: Payment integration
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to accept credit card payments in my WooCommerce store. Guide me through integrating Stripe, including creating a Stripe account, obtaining API keys, configuring the gateway, and testing the payment flow.

prompt_templates.title: Shipping configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure shipping for my WooCommerce store. I need to set up shipping zones for domestic and international orders, add flat rate and free shipping options, and integrate with shipping carriers.

prompt_templates.title: Full store customization and optimization
prompt_templates.prompt: Customize my WooCommerce store by modifying the product page templates, updating the cart and checkout styling, adding custom product fields, then optimize performance with caching, image optimization, database cleanup, and CDN setup. Finally, run end-to-end tests on the checkout flow.

output_examples.input: Set up WooCommerce store with Stripe payments
output: 1. Install WooCommerce plugin and complete setup wizard
2. Configure store address, currency, and tax settings
3. Create product categories and add sample products
4. Set up Stripe payment gateway with API keys
5. Test payment flow in sandbox mode
1. Enable WordPress caching plugin (WP Rocket or W3 Total Cache)
3. Clean up WooCommerce transients and expired data
5. Enable lazy loading for product images
6. Run performance audit and measure improvements

best_practices: Always test payment gateways in sandbox mode before going live
Back up your store before making configuration changes or installing extensions
Use SSL certificates for all WooCommerce stores handling payments
anti_patterns: Skipping payment flow testing before launch can result in lost sales
Installing too many extensions without performance consideration

faq.question: Do I need coding skills to use this workflow?
faq.answer: Basic WordPress knowledge is helpful but not required. The workflow guides you through configuration steps. Advanced customization may require PHP or CSS knowledge.

faq.question: Can this workflow integrate any payment gateway?
faq.answer: The workflow covers major gateways like Stripe and PayPal. Other gateways like Square, Authorize.net, or regional providers can be integrated using similar patterns.

faq.question: Is this workflow suitable for subscription products?
faq.answer: Yes, Phase 6 covers WooCommerce Subscriptions extension setup. You will need the WooCommerce Subscriptions plugin for recurring payment products.

faq.question: How do I test the store before going live?
faq.answer: Phase 8 covers comprehensive testing including checkout flows, payment processing, email notifications, and mobile experience. Use sandbox mode for payment testing.

faq.question: Can this workflow work with existing WooCommerce stores?
faq.answer: Yes, you can apply specific phases to existing stores. Use Phase 7 for optimization or Phase 3 for adding new payment methods to an established store.

faq.question: What hosting requirements does WooCommerce need?
faq.answer: WooCommerce requires WordPress hosting with PHP 7.4+, MySQL 5.6+, and HTTPS support. Recommended: 256MB+ memory limit, SSL certificate, and daily backups.
